commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3dd566ee3a98e8292275194f7b8096878044cb26)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes heartbeat enablement so per-agent heartbeat config merges with `agents.defaults.heartbeat` across runner, system-prompt, and ACP spawn paths, with docs, tests, and changelog updates.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main’s heartbeat summary and scheduler both use the explicit-only branch, and the existing regression test expects the default-inheriting agent to be disabled. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the contributor must refresh real behavior proof and maintainers must decide the empty-object heartbeat contract before any automated repair or merge path is appropriate.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): correct agent-level heartbeat fallback to respect defaults
target.body:
## Bug

`isHeartbeatEnabledForAgent` used an all-or-nothing check: if ANY agent in `agents.list` had an explicit `heartbeat` block, the function entered a mode where ONLY agents with their own heartbeat field were considered enabled. This silently ignored `agents.defaults.heartbeat` for agents without per-agent config.

Example config that triggers the bug:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "heartbeat": { "every": "60m", "target": "channel" }
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "main" },
      { "id": "ops", "heartbeat": { "every": "0m" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Expected:** `main` inherits the 60m default; `ops` is explicitly disabled.

**Actual:** `main` heartbeat is silently disabled because `ops` has a heartbeat block, flipping the function into "explicit-only" mode.

## Fix

Changes to standard override semantics across all heartbeat surfaces (runner, system-prompt, ACP spawn):

1. Per-agent heartbeat wins if present (including `every: "0m"` for opt-out).
2. Empty `heartbeat: {}` is treated as absent — falls through to defaults if they exist, or to legacy default-agent-only fallback if not.
3. If no per-agent heartbeat and no defaults, keep the legacy default-agent-only fallback.

`resolveHeartbeatAgents` in `heartbeat-runner.ts` is updated to match, using `isHeartbeatEnabledForAgent` per-agent instead of the old `hasExplicitHeartbeatAgents` gate. `heartbeat-system-prompt.ts` and `acp-spawn.ts` are also updated to use the unified `isHeartbeatEnabledForAgent` instead of their own local explicit-only gates. `docs/gateway/heartbeat.md` and `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` are updated to reflect the new semantics.

## Tests

Updated existing assertions and added coverage for:
- Mixed defaults + explicit: agents without per-agent config now correctly inherit defaults
- Explicit disabled (`every: "0m"`) honored even when defaults exist
- Empty heartbeat object `{}` treated as absent (falls through to defaults)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Agent-level heartbeat fallback incorrectly excluded default-inheriting agents when any other agent had an explicit heartbeat block. See bug section above for repro.

**Real environment tested:** openclaw source at commit `51a73181c2` (this PR head), Node 22.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /tmp/doncicx-openclaw
git checkout fix/heartbeat-agent-defaults-fallback
npx tsx proof.mts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Heartbeat enablement with defaults + per-agent overrides ===
Config: defaults.heartbeat.every = '30m'
  main:    no per-agent heartbeat  -> true (expects true — falls back to defaults)
  ops:     heartbeat.every = '0m' -> false (expects false — explicit opt-out)
  monitor: heartbeat: {}          -> true (expects true — empty treated as absent, falls back to defaults)

=== Without agents.defaults.heartbeat ===
  main: no per-agent heartbeat    -> true (expects true — legacy default-agent-only fallback)
  side: heartbeat: {}              -> false (expects false — empty without defaults, legacy default-agent-only)
```

*(Screenshot of terminal output will be added here)*

**Observed result after fix:** All 6 scenarios match expected behavior. `side` with `heartbeat: {}` and no defaults correctly returns `false`, fixing the P2 issue from prior review.

**What was not tested:** Long-running daemon heartbeat scheduling (only config parsing verified). ActiveHours filtering logic (not touched by this change). Actual cron job emission to channels (function-level unit test only).

Fixes #78713

@clawsweeper re-review
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
